Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Brian McMahon
Message:
feat(logging): v0.29.0 — SecretsRedactingFilter auto-attached to setup_logging chokepoint (#63)

Close 5/23-SF P0 sweep (o) at the institutional layer per
[[feedback_lift_invariants_to_chokepoint_after_second_recurrence]]:
alpha-engine-data #255 had shipped a repo-local API-key scrubber for
the FMP-/AWS-/Anthropic-key plaintext-log defect class. The 2026-05-24
audit on alpha-engine-research-runner CW Logs found the same defect
class in research (FMP API key visible in plaintext WARNING lines
during FMP /stable 402 paid-tier errors mid-Research). Second-occurrence
trigger fired → lift to lib chokepoint rather than per-repo mirror.

Implementation:

- New `SecretsRedactingFilter(logging.Filter)` in
  `alpha_engine_lib.logging` redacts known secret-shaped substrings
  from every log record reaching stdout / CW Logs / flow-doctor.
- Auto-attached to the StreamHandler created by `setup_logging()` by
  default. Opt-out via `ALPHA_ENGINE_DISABLE_LOG_REDACTION=1` env var
  (for the rare debugging scenario where a redaction regex obscures
  diagnosis of a non-secret pattern).
- Per-record opt-out via `record.no_redact = True` for known-safe
  emission sites (use rare).
- Filter NEVER raises — catch-all swallows any exception and lets the
  record pass through unmodified. A logging filter that crashes is
  worse than one that silently no-ops.

Pattern set (closed-form, conservative — false-positive-tolerant):

  - URL query credentials: `[?&]{apikey|api_key|key|token|access_token|
    auth_token}=<value≥16chars>` → `<REDACTED>`
  - AWS Access Key ID: `\bAKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}\b` → `<AWS_ACCESS_KEY_REDACTED>`
  - Anthropic keys: `\bsk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{16,}` → `<ANTHROPIC_KEY_REDACTED>`
  - OpenAI-style: `\bsk-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{32,}` → `<OPENAI_KEY_REDACTED>`
  - Bearer tokens: `authorization:\s*bearer\s+<token>` → header preserved,
    token `<REDACTED>`
  - GitHub PATs: `ghp_/gho_/ghu_/ghs_/ghr_` classic + `github_pat_`
    fine-grained → `<GITHUB_TOKEN_REDACTED>`

Tests (14 new in test_logging.py, all passing):

  - Per-pattern redaction (FMP URL, args-substitution, AWS, Anthropic,
    OpenAI, Bearer, GitHub classic + fine-grained)
  - Short-token-below-floor not redacted (false-positive avoidance)
  - No-match-unchanged (don't mutate clean records)
  - Filter never raises on malformed record
  - Per-record opt-out via record.no_redact
  - setup_logging attaches filter by default
  - setup_logging opt-out via ALPHA_ENGINE_DISABLE_LOG_REDACTION
  - End-to-end: setup_logging emit goes through redaction (capsys)

Full lib suite 702 pass.

Consumers (research / data / predictor / backtester) inherit the filter
automatically on their next lib pin bump — no per-repo code changes.
First consumer migration: alpha-engine-research pin bump v0.X → v0.29.0
(closes (o) the moment the Lambda runtime picks up the new pin). Other
repos pin on their natural cycles; alpha-engine-data's local scrubber
from #255 can be retired in a follow-on P1.

Version: 0.28.2 → 0.29.0 (pyproject.toml + __init__.py lockstep — pinned
against drift by existing test_init_version_matches_pyproject regression).

Composes with [[feedback_no_silent_fails]] (security defaults active by
default, opt-out not opt-in) + [[feedback_sota_institutional_default_no_shortcuts]]
(chokepoint lift on second recurrence is the institutional pattern).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Brian McMahon
Message:
feat(alerts): dedup_key + dedup_window_min — one alert per event (v0.24.0) (#55)

Lift the alert-storm fix from cost_report.py (alpha-engine-backtester
#239) to the institutional layer so every caller — cost_report,
research:score_aggregator, the 8 L221 canary alert sites, and any
future failure-surveillance call — gets dedup with a single
parameter.

Public API additions to ``alpha_engine_lib.alerts.publish``:

  * ``dedup_key: str | None = None`` — opaque caller-chosen string.
    Same key + same window ⇒ at most one publish per window.
  * ``dedup_window_min: int | None = 60`` — minutes; None = forever.
  * ``dedup_bucket: str | None = None`` — defaults to
    ``alpha-engine-research``.

PublishResult grows ``dedup_skipped: bool`` + ``dedup_reason: str``.
``any_ok`` returns True on dedup-skip (the alert is logically in the
operator's hands via the earlier publish).

CLI parallel: ``--dedup-key`` + ``--dedup-window-min`` (0 = forever)
+ ``--dedup-bucket``. Bash callers:

    python -m alpha_engine_lib.alerts publish \\
      --message "..." --severity error --source "..." \\
      --dedup-key "canary-rollback-$(date -u +%Y%m%d%H)"

S3 marker layout: ``s3://{bucket}/_alerts/_dedup/{sha1[:16]}.json``.
Read-modify-write so ``first_published_at`` is preserved across
window refreshes; ``publish_count`` grows monotonically.

Fail-safe throughout: any S3 error on the check falls through to
publish (better an extra alert than silent drop). Marker write
failure after a successful publish is logged but never propagates.
A failed publish (both channels) does NOT write the marker — so
retries on recovered backends still fire.

Backwards-compat: parameters are optional; old callers behave
unchanged. v0.23.0 → v0.24.0 (additive minor).

22 new tests pin: deterministic key derivation, NoSuchKey/expired/
within-window/None-window semantics, ClientError + corrupt-marker
fail-safe, RMW preservation of first_published_at + publish_count
increment, write-failure-swallowed, end-to-end suppress + expire +
None-disables-dedup + failed-publish-no-marker, CLI flag wiring +
``--dedup-window-min 0`` ⇒ forever, dedup-skipped stderr line.
Full suite 555 → 577.

Consumer migrations queued for next session: cost_report (replace
local helpers in #239 with lib call), research:score_aggregator
(_check_pillar_distribution_sanity), L221 canary alerts (the 4 open
PRs + their 8 sites).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
